info.name: Migration Playground â€” Sprint 1
info.description:
API test collection for the Oracle â†’ PostgreSQL Migration Playground backend.

Covers the Sprint 1 upload endpoint (API-01 to API-04).

Base URL: http://localhost:8080

Import this file into Postman (File â†’ Import) to instantly have all requests ready to run.

variable.description: Base URL of the running Spring Boot backend. Change this if your server runs on a different port.

item.name: Sprint 1 â€” Upload Endpoint
item.description: All four test cases for POST /api/v1/migrations/upload

formdata.description: A valid Oracle DDL .sql file. Use the sample from sql/samples/ in this repo.

request.description:
**API-01 â€” Happy Path**

Uploads a valid Oracle DDL `.sql` file. The backend parses every `CREATE TABLE` block, counts tables and columns, persists a `MigrationRun` record to PostgreSQL, and returns it.

**Expected response:** `201 Created`
```json
{
  "id": "<uuid>",
  "fileName": "sample_01_basic_ddl.sql",
  "status": "PARSED",
  "tableCount": <n>,
  "columnCount": <n>,
  "createdAt": "<iso-timestamp>"
}
```

item.name: API-02 | Upload with custom file_name override

query.description: Optional. Overrides the original file name stored in the MigrationRun record.

request.description:
**API-02 â€” Custom file_name override**

Same as API-01 but supplies the optional `file_name` query parameter. The `MigrationRun` record stored in the DB will use `my_custom_name.sql` instead of the original file name.

This is useful when the client renames the file before uploading, or when sending the file programmatically.

**Expected response:** `201 Created` with `fileName` = `my_custom_name.sql`

item.name: API-03 | Upload non-.sql file â†’ 400 Bad Request

formdata.description: A non-.sql file (e.g. a .md, .txt, or .csv). The backend must reject this.

request.description:
**API-03 â€” Invalid file type**

Uploads a file that does not have a `.sql` extension. The `SchemaService` guard rejects it immediately before any parsing occurs.

**Expected response:** `400 Bad Request`
```json
{
  "timestamp": "<iso-timestamp>",
  "status": 400,
  "error": "BAD_REQUEST",
  "message": "Uploaded file is not a valid SQL file.",
  "path": "/api/v1/migrations/upload"
}
```

response.name: 400 Bad Request â€” Invalid file extension
response.body:
{
  "timestamp": "2026-04-12T11:49:00Z",
  "status": 400,
  "error": "BAD_REQUEST",
  "message": "Uploaded file is not a valid SQL file.",
  "path": "/api/v1/migrations/upload"
}

item.name: API-04 | Upload oversized file â†’ 413 Payload Too Large

formdata.description: Attach any file larger than 10MB. The Spring Boot multipart limit will reject it before it reaches the controller.

request.description:
**API-04 â€” File exceeds 10MB limit**

The `application.properties` sets `spring.servlet.multipart.max-file-size=10MB`. When this is exceeded, Spring Boot throws `MaxUploadSizeExceededException` before the request even reaches the controller. The `GlobalExceptionHandler` catches it and returns a structured 413 response.

**Expected response:** `413 Payload Too Large`
```json
{
  "timestamp": "<iso-timestamp>",
  "status": 413,
  "error": "PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE",
  "message": "File size exceeds the 10MB limit.",
  "path": "/api/v1/migrations/upload"
}
```

response.name: 413 Payload Too Large â€” File exceeds 10MB

item.name: Sprint 2 â€” Analysis Endpoints
item.description: Endpoints to run and retrieve compatibility analysis (API-05 and API-06)

request.description:
**API-05 â€” Run Analysis**

Runs the compatibility analyzer for the given Migration Run ID.

**API-06 â€” Get Analysis**

Retrieves the generated compatibility analysis report.
